title: Getting started with tinytrail
text: Place two calls every script trail stays current automatically. Call tinytrail() top script short description script : wrap file path argument save call tinytrail_write(). logs path returns unchanged, slots directly file = argument write function: works equally well ::(): need add. trail file (_tinytrail.yaml) created project root first run updated every subsequent run:
code: library(tinytrail)  # --- top of 01_clean.R --- tinytrail(   description = "Clean and reshape survey data",   data_source = "Current Population Survey (BLS)" ) write.csv(   survey,   file = tinytrail_write("data/clean/survey_clean.csv") ) write.csv(   survey,   file = tinytrail_write(here::here("data/clean/survey_clean.csv")) ) $version: 0.1.0 $learn_more: https://github.com/tinytrail-r/tinytrail scripts:   01_clean.R:     description: Clean and reshape survey data     data_source: Current Population Survey (BLS)     first_run: '2026-01-15 09:12'     latest_run: '2026-01-20 11:05'     script_runtime: 0.3 min     n_outputs: 1     outputs:     - data/clean/survey_clean.csv   02_model.R:     description: Fit logistic regression     data_source: Cleaned survey data (01_clean.R)     first_run: '2026-01-16 14:22'     latest_run: '2026-01-20 11:08'     script_runtime: 1.4 min     n_outputs: 2     outputs:     - results/model_fit.rds     - results/table_coefficients.csv

title: Getting started with tinytrail
text: tinytrail_dict() optional third function. Place end read clean pipeline capture column names sample values input data frame: entry stored calling script trail file: omit sample values record column names, use sample_values = FALSE:
code: survey <- read.csv("data/raw/survey.csv") |>   tinytrail_dict() data_dictionary:   01_clean.R:     survey:       columns:         id: [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]         age: [34, 52, 28, 41, 37]         response: ['yes', 'no', 'yes', 'yes', 'no'] survey <- read.csv("data/raw/survey.csv") |>   tinytrail_dict(sample_values = FALSE)

title: Getting started with tinytrail
text: Pin important script top trail — useful main.R sources scripts:
code: tinytrail(   description = "Master data preparation",   data_source = "Admin registry (Statistics Sweden)",   pin_to_top  = TRUE )

title: Authors and Citation
text: Tomas Reivinger. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Reivinger T (2026). tinytrail: Lightweight Auto-updating Project Tracker Data Dictionary. R package version 0.1.0, https://github.com/tinytrail-r/tinytrail.
code: @Manual{,   title = {tinytrail: Lightweight Auto-updating Project Tracker and Data Dictionary},   author = {Tomas Reivinger},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/tinytrail-r/tinytrail}, }

what: Lightweight Auto-updating Project Tracker and Data Dictionary
title: Lightweight Auto-updating Project Tracker and Data Dictionary
text: tinytrail lightweight R package — initialized — leaves ‘tiny trail’ human- AI-readable information script saves disk, making effortless keep better track small medium-sized projects. maintains YAML trail file (_tinytrail.yaml) project root recording scripts produced output files script’s runtime. tinytrail package also provides convenience function effortlessly registers column names along sample values (optional) YAML.

title: Lightweight Auto-updating Project Tracker and Data Dictionary
code: # Development version from GitHub: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("tinytrail-r/tinytrail")

title: Lightweight Auto-updating Project Tracker and Data Dictionary
text: Place tinytrail() near top script wrap save calls tinytrail_write(): tinytrail() automatically captures file name creates updates _tinytrail.yaml: capturing work? tinytrail() uses R’s built-trace() mechanism temporarily observe common write functions — write.csv(), saveRDS(), readr::write_csv(), ggplot2::ggsave(), — duration script. one called, file path quietly noted function runs normal. behaviour changed files modified. hooks removed automatically script exits, leaving R session exactly . write functions built-list, pass data.frame extra_hooks function name file-path argument: (two already captured automatically — ’re shown illustration .) can also fall back wrapping path manually tinytrail_write() anything covered. Since tinytrail_write() just thin wrapper works expected. Optionally, pipe data frames tinytrail_dict() capture column names sample values: adds data dictionary entry _tinytrail.yaml:
code: library(tinytrail) library(ggplot2) library(tinytable)  tinytrail(   description = "Summarise survey responses by age group",   data_source = "Current Population Survey (BLS)" )  dat <- read.csv("data/raw/survey.csv")    # wrap the path with tinytrail_write() to register the output ggplot(dat) +   aes(x = age, fill = response) +   geom_histogram() |>   ggsave(file = tinytrail_write("output/fig1.tex"))  # same for tables lm(age ~ response, data = dat) |>   summary() |>   coef() |>   tt(digits = 3) |>   save_tt(file = tinytrail_write("output/tab1.tex")) scripts:   01_clean.R:     description: Summarise survey responses by age group     data_source: Current Population Survey (BLS)     first_run: '2026-06-27 09:00'     latest_run: '2026-06-27 09:01'     script_runtime: 0.2 min     n_outputs: 2     outputs:     - output/fig1.tex     - output/tab1.tex tinytrail(   description = "Export results",   extra_hooks = data.frame(     fn  = c("readr::write_csv", "ggplot2::ggsave"),     arg = c("file",             "filename")   ) ) ggsave( file = tinytrail_write( here::here("output/tab1.tex") )) # ... cleaning and preparing data ...  dat |>   tinytrail_dict() data_dictionary:   01_clean.R:     dat:       columns:         id: [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]         age: [34, 52, 28, 41, 37]         response: ['yes', 'no', 'yes', 'yes', 'no']

what: Register a script in the project trail — tinytrail
title: Register a script in the project trail — tinytrail
text: Call near top every script. Creates updates entry _tinytrail.yaml sets script name outputs can recorded.

title: Register a script in the project trail — tinytrail
code: tinytrail(   description,   data_source = NULL,   pin_to_top = FALSE,   record_runtime = TRUE,   name = NULL,   auto = TRUE,   extra_hooks = NULL )

title: Register a script in the project trail — tinytrail
text: description Character. Description script . data_source Character. Optional. Enter sources data used script — name dataset survey, file path (e.g. "Current Population Survey (BLS)"). pin_to_top Logical. Pin script top trail. Useful main.R sources scripts — keeps visible top _tinytrail.yaml regardless alphabetical order. Default FALSE. record_runtime Logical. Record elapsed time exit. Default TRUE. name Character. Override auto-detected script name. Useful testing auto-detection available. auto Logical. Automatically intercept common write functions record output paths. Default TRUE. Set FALSE use explicit tinytrail_write() calls instead. extra_hooks data.frame columns fn arg specifying additional write functions intercept auto = TRUE. fn function name ("my_save" script-level function, "pkg::fn" package function). arg name file-path argument function. Functions packages installed silently skipped.

title: Register a script in the project trail — tinytrail
text: name (script name), invisibly. Called side effect creating updating YAML trail file project root.

title: Register a script in the project trail — tinytrail
text: default (auto = TRUE) common write functions (write.csv, saveRDS, readr::write_csv, ggplot2::ggsave, etc.) hooked automatically output file paths captured without tinytrail_write() wrapper. Set auto = FALSE use explicit tinytrail_write() calls instead.

title: Register a script in the project trail — tinytrail
code: # \donttest{ withr::with_tempdir({   writeLines("Version: 1.0", "DESCRIPTION")   withr::with_options(     list(.tinytrail_registry_path = NULL, .tinytrail_current_script = NULL,          .tinytrail_traced_fns = NULL, .tinytrail_hooks_table = NULL), {      # auto = TRUE (default): write.csv captured without tinytrail_write()     tinytrail(       description    = "Clean and reshape survey data",       data_source    = "Current Population Survey (BLS)",       record_runtime = FALSE,       name           = "clean.R"     )     write.csv(mtcars, "cars.csv")      # extra_hooks: add a function not in the built-in list     tinytrail(       description    = "Export final tables",       record_runtime = FALSE,       name           = "export.R",       extra_hooks    = data.frame(fn = "tinytable::save_tt", arg = "output")     )      # auto = FALSE: use explicit tinytrail_write() wrappers     tinytrail(       description    = "Sources and runs all project scripts in order",       pin_to_top     = TRUE,       record_runtime = FALSE,       auto           = FALSE,       name           = "main.R"     )   }) }) # }

what: Add a data frame to the project data dictionary — tinytrail_dict
title: Add a data frame to the project data dictionary — tinytrail_dict
text: Place end read/clean pipeline capture column names optionally sample values. Returns data frame unchanged.

title: Add a data frame to the project data dictionary — tinytrail_dict
code: tinytrail_dict(   df,   .name = NULL,   sample_values = TRUE,   sample_string_length = 18L )

title: Add a data frame to the project data dictionary — tinytrail_dict
text: df data frame. .name Character. Label entry. Defaults variable name df written calling code (e.g. mtcars |> tinytrail_dict() records "mtcars"). Override expression simple name need custom label. sample_values Logical. Record 5 sample values per column. Default TRUE. sample_string_length Integer Inf. Maximum characters per sample value truncating "...". Default 18L.

title: Add a data frame to the project data dictionary — tinytrail_dict
text: df, invisibly.

title: Add a data frame to the project data dictionary — tinytrail_dict
text: Requires tinytrail() called first session.

title: Add a data frame to the project data dictionary — tinytrail_dict
code: # \donttest{ withr::with_tempdir({   writeLines("Version: 1.0", "DESCRIPTION")   withr::with_options(     list(.tinytrail_registry_path = NULL, .tinytrail_current_script = NULL), {      tinytrail("Analyse mtcars", name = "analysis.R", record_runtime = FALSE)     dat <- mtcars |> tinytrail_dict(.name = "cars")   }) }) # }

what: Record an output file path in the trail — tinytrail_write
title: Record an output file path in the trail — tinytrail_write
text: Wraps file path argument save call. Registers path current script's trail entry returns path unchanged, can dropped inline save function.

title: Record an output file path in the trail — tinytrail_write
Record an output file path in the trail — tinytrail_write
text: file Character. Path output file.

title: Record an output file path in the trail — tinytrail_write
text: file, invisibly.

title: Record an output file path in the trail — tinytrail_write
text: Requires tinytrail() called first session.

title: Record an output file path in the trail — tinytrail_write
code: # \donttest{ withr::with_tempdir({   writeLines("Version: 1.0", "DESCRIPTION")   withr::with_options(     list(.tinytrail_registry_path = NULL, .tinytrail_current_script = NULL), {      tinytrail("Process raw data", name = "analysis.R", record_runtime = FALSE)     out <- tinytrail_write("output/results.csv")   }) }) # }

title: tinytrail 0.1.0
text: Initial release (renamed tinylog). tinytrail() registers script project YAML trail optionally records elapsed runtime. tinytrail_write() wraps save call log output file path calling script’s trail entry. tinytrail_dict() captures column names sample values input data frames.
